    Configure: Events, Key Events, Audiences, Custom Dimensions</div><h3>Key Reports</h3><ul><li><strong>Traffic Acquisition:</strong> Sessions by channel (Organic, Paid, Direct, Referral)</li><li><strong>User Acquisition:</strong> Where new users came from (first touch)</li><li><strong>Pages and Screens:</strong> Most viewed pages + engagement time</li><li><strong>Events:</strong> All events with counts</li><li><strong>Ecommerce Purchases:</strong> Revenue, items sold</li></ul><div class="tip-box"><strong>💡 Always compare to prior period.</strong> Raw numbers mean nothing without context.</div>`},
    ga_events:{platform:'GOOGLE ANALYTICS — BEGINNER',title:'Events, Parameters & Key Events',body:`<h3>Auto-Collected Events</h3><p>GA4 tracks automatically: page_view, session_start, first_visit, scroll (90%), click (outbound), file_download, video events (YouTube embeds).</p><h3>Recommended Events</h3><p>Google-defined events with specific schemas: purchase, add_to_cart, begin_checkout, generate_lead, sign_up, search, view_item.</p><div class="code-block">// GA4 Purchase Event
    gtag('event', 'purchase', {
    transaction_id: "T12345",
    value: 999.00,
    currency: "INR",
    items: [{
    item_id: "SKU001",
    item_name: "Running Shoes",
    price: 999.00,
    quantity: 1
    }]
    });</div><h3>Key Events</h3><p>Configure → Events → Toggle "Mark as key event." These appear highlighted in reports and can be imported into Google Ads as conversion actions.</p>`},
    ga_reports:{platform:'GOOGLE ANALYTICS — BEGINNER',title:'Standard Reports Overview',body:`<h3>Acquisition Reports</h3><p><strong>Traffic Acquisition:</strong> Sessions by default channel grouping (Organic, Paid Search, Paid Social, Direct, Referral, Email).<br><strong>User Acquisition:</strong> New users by first user source — shows where new customers come from.</p><h3>Engagement Reports</h3><ul><li><strong>Engagement Rate:</strong> Sessions lasting 10+ sec, with key event, or 2+ pages</li><li><strong>Average Engagement Time:</strong> Replaces Session Duration</li></ul><h3>UTM Parameters — Essential</h3><div class="code-block">Tag all paid links:
    utm_source=facebook
    utm_medium=paid_social
    utm_campaign=diwali_sale_2024
    utm_content=video_v1
    utm_term=running_shoes (paid search only)</div><div class="warn-box"><strong>⚠️ NEVER run paid campaigns without UTM tags.</strong> Without them, traffic shows as "Direct" and you lose all attribution.</div>`},
    ga_gtm:{platform:'GOOGLE ANALYTICS — BEGINNER',title:'GA4 with Google Tag Manager',body:`<h3>Why GTM?</h3><p>Add/modify GA4 tracking without editing website code. Save developer time, let marketers own tracking.</p><h3>GTM Setup</h3><div class="code-block">1. Create GTM account → Install container code
    2. New Tag → GA4 Configuration
    → Enter G-XXXXXXXX Measurement ID
    → Trigger: All Pages
    (replaces the GA4 snippet)
    3. For each custom event:
    New Tag → GA4 Event
    → Config: your GA4 tag
    → Event Name: purchase / lead / etc.
    → Parameters: from Data Layer
    → Trigger: your conversion trigger</div><h3>Data Layer</h3><p>JavaScript object your website populates with structured data (product details, transaction ID). GTM reads it and sends to GA4. Work with developers to push relevant data on conversion pages.</p>`},
    ga_funnels:{platform:'GOOGLE ANALYTICS — INTERMEDIATE',title:'Funnel & Path Exploration',body:`<h3>Funnel Exploration</h3><p>Visualize user progression through a conversion sequence and see drop-off rates at each step.</p><div class="code-block">Example eCommerce Funnel:
    Step 1: view_item (product page)
    Step 2: add_to_cart
    Step 3: begin_checkout
    Step 4: add_payment_info
    Step 5: purchase
    Insight: 60% drop at checkout
    → Investigate: too many steps? payment options? trust?</div><h3>Path Exploration</h3><p>Shows pages users visit before/after a specific page. Set "ending point" as purchase confirmation to see which paths lead to conversion.</p><div class="tip-box"><strong>💡 Segment Funnels:</strong> Apply Mobile vs Desktop segment. You'll often find wildly different conversion rates that reveal where to focus.</div>`},
    ga_segments:{platform:'GOOGLE ANALYTICS — INTERMEDIATE',title:'Segments & Comparisons',body:`<h3>Segment Types</h3><div class="code-block">User Segments: Users meeting criteria across all sessions
    Session Segments: Sessions where criteria is met
    Event Segments: Events matching specific parameters</div><h3>Powerful Segment Ideas</h3><ul><li>Converters vs Non-converters — what's different?</li><li>Mobile vs Desktop — where is CVR lower?</li><li>Paid Social vs Organic Search — behaviour differences?</li><li>Users who watched video vs didn't — does video affect CVR?</li><li>High-value vs low-value buyers — different acquisition channels?</li></ul><h3>Comparisons (Standard Reports)</h3><p>Add up to 4 comparisons (e.g., Device Type = Mobile vs Desktop) to compare metrics side by side without building a full Exploration.</p>`},
    ga_ecommerce:{platform:'GOOGLE ANALYTICS — INTERMEDIATE',title:'eCommerce Tracking in GA4',body:`<h3>GA4 Ecommerce Events</h3><div class="code-block">view_item_list (category/search results)
    view_item (product detail page)
    add_to_cart
    view_cart
    begin_checkout
    add_payment_info
    purchase (must include transaction_id, value, items[])
    refund (for returns)</div><h3>Revenue Accuracy</h3><p>The purchase event is most critical. Ensure: unique transaction_id per order, accurate value (revenue excluding tax/shipping), correct currency. Inaccurate values corrupt tROAS bidding.</p><div class="warn-box"><strong>⚠️ Test thoroughly:</strong> Place a real test order and verify in GTM Preview + GA4 DebugView. Wrong values in production = wrong Smart Bidding targets.</div>`},
    ga_googleads:{platform:'GOOGLE ANALYTICS — INTERMEDIATE',title:'GA4 ↔ Google Ads Integration',body:`<h3>Linking</h3><div class="code-block">In GA4: Admin → Google Ads Links → Link
    In Google Ads: Tools → Conversions → Import → GA4
    Linking enables:
    ✓ Import GA4 Key Events as Google Ads conversions
    ✓ GA4 audiences in Google Ads
    ✓ Google Ads data in GA4 acquisition reports
    ✓ Auto-tagging (GCLID) attribution</div><h3>Importing Conversions</h3><p>Use GA4 Key Events as your Google Ads conversion source for a single source of truth. Choose: "One per click" for purchases, "Every conversion" for lead forms.</p><div class="tip-box"><strong>💡 Auto-Tagging:</strong> Google Ads Settings → Auto-Tagging ON. Appends GCLID to all clicks, letting GA4 attribute sessions to specific campaigns and keywords.</div>`},
    ga_audiences:{platform:'GOOGLE ANALYTICS — INTERMEDIATE',title:'Building Audiences in GA4',body:`<h3>Audience Builder</h3><p>Configure → Audiences → New Audience. Build based on events, parameters, user properties, demographics. Publish to Google Ads for targeting.</p><div class="code-block">Key Audiences to Build:
    1. All Converters (purchased last 30 days)
    → Exclude from prospecting, use for cross-sell
    2. High-Intent Non-Converters
    → add_to_cart + no purchase in 7 days
    → Retarget with offer
    3. Top Engaged Users
    → Session &gt;3 min + 3+ pages
    → Lookalike source
    4. Churned Customers
    → Purchased 90–180 days ago, no recent visit
    → Win-back campaigns</div><div class="tip-box"><strong>💡 Freshness:</strong> Audiences update daily. Max membership duration: 540 days.</div>`},
    ga_attribution_ga:{platform:'GOOGLE ANALYTICS — ADVANCED',title:'Attribution in GA4',body:`<h3>Attribution Settings</h3><p>Admin → Attribution Settings → Choose reporting attribution model. Affects credit in GA4 reports (not platform dashboards).</p><div class="code-block">Available Models:
    Data-Driven Attribution (default, recommended)
    Last Click
    First Click
    Linear
    Time Decay
    Position-Based
    Cross-channel last click (ignores direct traffic)</div><h3>Advertising Workspace</h3><p>GA4's Advertising section includes the Model Comparison Tool — compare conversion counts under different attribution models side by side. Essential for understanding cross-channel credit.</p>`},
    ga_bigquery:{platform:'GOOGLE ANALYTICS — ADVANCED',title:'GA4 + BigQuery Export',body:`<h3>Why BigQuery?</h3><p>GA4 standard reports have sampling limitations. BigQuery gives raw unsampled event-level data, unlimited SQL queries, join with CRM data, and permanent storage.</p><div class="code-block">Setup:
    1. Create Google Cloud project
    2. GA4 Admin → BigQuery Links → Link
    3. Choose: Daily or Streaming export
    4. Data appears as events_YYYYMMDD tables
    Sample Query:
    SELECT page_location, SUM(purchase_revenue) as revenue
    FROM \`project.dataset.events_*\`
    WHERE event_name = 'purchase'
    GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC</div><div class="tip-box"><strong>💡 Cost:</strong> BigQuery free tier: 10GB storage + 1TB queries/month. Most SMBs stay within free tier easily.</div>`},
    ga_custom_dimensions:{platform:'GOOGLE ANALYTICS — ADVANCED',title:'Custom Dimensions & Metrics',body:`<h3>What Are Custom Dimensions?</h3><p>Additional data you send with events that GA4 doesn't collect by default. For business-specific analysis: user type, subscription plan, product category, content author.</p><div class="code-block">User-scope example:
    gtag('set', 'user_properties', {
    user_type: 'premium',
    account_age: '2_years'
    });
    View remainder of file in raw view
